summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments in small UAS operations. Ukraine has tested and deployed dual-control FPV drones capable of switching between fiber optic and radio links when the cable fails, with one such drone downing a Russian Ka-52 helicopter near Pokrovsk, reportedly worth $16 million. The U.S. Air Force Special Operations Command is seeking backpack-portable FPV drones, while Ukraine's military continues to refine tactics for targeting helicopters despite rotor turbulence challenges. Separately, Iranian-backed forces used FPV drones to strike a U.S. Black Hawk helicopter and NASAMS radar at an Iraqi base, and Russian FPV drones have killed civilians in Kharkiv, including a man in a car in Zolochiv and a passenger on an electric train.
